(2 R ,4 R , E )-2,4-Dihydroxy-8-iodo-7-methyloct-7-en-1-yl 2,4,6-triisopropylbenzenesulfonate ( 26a ) To a flame-dried round-bottom flask was added DMAP (190.6 mg, 1.56 mmol, 0.2 equiv), Et3N (1.18 g, 11.7 mmol, 1.5 equiv), and 25a (2.34 g, 7.80 mmol, 1.0 equiv) in CH2Cl2 (21 mL). The mixture was subsequently cooled to −78 °C and a solution of 2,4,6-triisopropylsulfonyl chloride (9.45 g, 31.2 mmol, 4.0 equiv) dissolved in CH2Cl2 (10 mL) was added dropwise. The reaction was slowly warmed to −20 °C, at which temperature it was stirred over the next 22 h. The reaction mixture was quenched with NaHCO3 (sat. aq.). The aqueous layer was extracted with CH2Cl2, and dried over Na2SO4. The residue was purified by flash column chromatography (n-hexane/EtOAc = 2:1, v/v) on silica to afford the product 26a (4.32 g, 7.63 mmol, 98%) as a white solid. [α]D25 = +4.00 (CHCl3, c = 0.5); mp: 104–106 °C;
